title: Prune stale immutable ConfigMaps after deployment
summary: All three items from berendt's external review (W-001, W-002, W-003) are correctly addressed. Known limitation for pre-existing ConfigMaps is documented in godoc and reference docs. Integration test now asserts absence of oldest ConfigMap. New E2E Chainsaw test covers deploy, 4 sequential config changes, DeploymentReady waits, and ConfigMap count assertion. All unit tests pass, go vet clean, no lint issues.

tests_checklist.item: Integration test asserts absence of oldest ConfigMap (W-003 fix)
E2E Chainsaw test exists for config-pruning (W-002 fix)
Edge cases covered (retain=0, no candidates, overlapping prefix)

code_quality_checklist.item: Known limitation documented in reference docs (kubernetes-packages.md:190)
Review pattern created for label-based selector backward compatibility
Existing review patterns updated with CC-0077 occurrences

security_checklist.item: Owner UID check prevents unauthorized deletion (config.go:249-251)
Label selector uses controller-runtime API (no injection vector)

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible
W-001 addressed via documentation (team decision: known limitation)
E2E test follows established Chainsaw pattern

defensive_checklist.item: All external inputs validated (owner UID, prefix, currentName exclusion)
NotFound errors handled gracefully for concurrent reconcile safety

suggested_improvements: The idempotency integration test (integration_test.go:187-236) does not track firstCreatedName or assert its absence, unlike the prune test (line 184). Adding g.Expect(remaining).NotTo(ContainElement(firstCreatedName)) would strengthen the idempotency assertion to verify WHICH ConfigMaps survived, not just the count. This is a pre-existing gap not introduced in this diff, so it is a suggestion only.

Documentation algorithm step 1 (kubernetes-packages.md:154) says 'Lists all ConfigMaps in the namespace' but the implementation uses client.MatchingLabels as a server-side selector. Updating to 'Lists ConfigMaps matching the forge.c5c3.io/config-base label' would be more precise and make the pre-existing limitation self-evident from the algorithm description. This line was not changed in this diff.
next_steps: Merge — all blocking and major checks pass, external review feedback fully addressed
